messages.content: Recent works have explored moving reasoning from discrete token sequences to a continuous latent space, so models can \textit{think} with hidden states or learned embeddings rather than emitting long chain-of-thought text. Training LLMs to reason in latent space ~\citep{hao2024training,shen2025codi,wei2025sim} has been shown to improve reasoning efficiency and flexibility. Inspired by these promising advances, recent works empower multimodal reasoning through 在传统生成自然语言之前，将special latent tokens in continuous semantic space in MLLMs 和auxiliary helper images~\citep{yang2025machine} 或者 annotated bounding boxes~\citep{li2025latent} 的 visual embeddings，把“看见什么”先变成一个更接近语言模型可消费的、语义一致的中间表示，从而让后续的推理过程更稳、更准、更可控。 翻译并润色

Recent works have explored shifting reasoning from discrete token sequences to a continuous latent space, enabling models to *think* with hidden states or learned embeddings instead of emitting long chains of thought. Training LLMs to reason in latent space \citep{hao2024training,shen2025codi,wei2025sim} has been shown to improve reasoning efficiency and flexibility. Building on these advances, more recent studies extend latent-space reasoning to multimodal settings: before generating natural language, MLLMs first introduce special latent tokens in a continuous semantic space, or incorporate visual embeddings from auxiliary helper images \citep{yang2025machine} or annotated bounding boxes \citep{li2025latent}. This effectively turns “what is seen” into an intermediate representation that is more semantically aligned and more readily consumable by language models, thereby making subsequent reasoning more stable, accurate, and controllable.
不要用：
你想让我避免使用哪些词/表达/写法？请把“不要用：”后面的具体内容发我（例如某些词、某种句式、不要斜体、不要英文、不要被动语态等），我再按要求重译并润色。
Building on these advances, more recent studies extend latent-space reasoning to multimodal settings: before generating natural language

在此基础上，近期研究将潜空间推理进一步拓展到多模态场景：在生成自然语言之前，模型会先引入位于连续语义空间的特殊潜在标记（latent tokens），或融合来自辅助图像（auxiliary helper images）\citep{yang2025machine}、以及标注边界框（annotated bounding boxes）\citep{li2025latent}的视觉嵌入。这样可将“看见什么”先转化为一种与语言模型更语义一致、也更易被其消费的中间表示，从而使后续推理过程更稳、更准且更可控。
Building on these advances, more recent studies extend latent-space reasoning to multimodal settings: before generating natural language 不要用：

在此基础上，近期研究将潜空间推理推广至多模态场景：在输出自然语言之前，模型会先引入连续语义空间中的特殊潜在标记（latent tokens），或融合来自辅助图像（auxiliary helper images）\citep{yang2025machine}与标注边界框（annotated bounding boxes）\citep{li2025latent}的视觉嵌入，将“看见什么”预先转化为语义对齐、便于语言模型处理的中间表示，从而提升后续推理的稳定性、准确性与可控性。
英语

On the basis of these advances, recent studies have pushed latent-space reasoning into multimodal settings: before producing natural-language outputs, MLLMs first construct special latent tokens in a continuous semantic space, or incorporate visual embeddings derived from auxiliary helper images \citep{yang2025machine} or annotated bounding boxes \citep{li2025latent}. This converts “what is seen” into an intermediate representation that is better aligned with the language model’s semantics and easier to process, making subsequent reasoning more stable, accurate, and controllable.

Recent works have explored shifting reasoning from discrete token sequences to a continuous latent space, enabling models to _think_ with hidden states or learned embeddings instead of emitting long chains of thought. Training LLMs to reason in latent space \citep{hao2024training,shen2025codi,wei2025sim} has been shown to improve reasoning efficiency and flexibility. Building on these advances, more recent studies extend latent-space reasoning to multimodal settings: before generating natural language, MLLMs first introduce special latent tokens in a continuous semantic space, or incorporate visual embeddings from auxiliary helper images \citep{yang2025machine} or annotated bounding boxes \citep{li2025latent}. This effectively turns “what is seen” into an intermediate representation that is more semantically aligned and more readily consumable by language models, thereby making subsequent reasoning more stable, accurate, and controllable. 重新润色

Recent work has investigated shifting reasoning from discrete token sequences to a continuous latent space, allowing models to *think* with hidden states or learned embeddings rather than emitting lengthy chains of thought. Training LLMs to reason in latent space \citep{hao2024training,shen2025codi,wei2025sim} has been shown to improve both efficiency and flexibility. Following this line of research, newer approaches extend latent-space reasoning to multimodal settings: before producing natural-language outputs, MLLMs introduce special latent tokens in a continuous semantic space, or leverage visual embeddings from auxiliary helper images \citep{yang2025machine} or annotated bounding boxes \citep{li2025latent}. In doing so, they convert “what is seen” into an intermediate representation that is semantically better aligned with language models and easier for them to process, leading to more stable, accurate, and controllable reasoning.

这里理解错了，在生成自然语言之前，MLLM先将special latent tokens in a continuous semantic space 与 visual embedding对齐（这个visual embedding可能来自auxiliary helper images \citep{yang2025machine} or annotated bounding boxes \citep{li2025latent}）

Recent work has investigated shifting reasoning from discrete token sequences to a continuous latent space, allowing models to *think* with hidden states or learned embeddings rather than emitting lengthy chains of thought. Training LLMs to reason in latent space \citep{hao2024training,shen2025codi,wei2025sim} has been shown to improve both efficiency and flexibility. Extending this idea to multimodal settings, recent methods train MLLMs to align special latent tokens in a continuous semantic space with visual embeddings—where the visual embeddings may come from auxiliary helper images \citep{yang2025machine} or annotated bounding boxes \citep{li2025latent}—before generating natural language. This alignment transforms “what is seen” into an intermediate, semantically coherent representation that language models can more readily consume, making downstream reasoning more stable, accurate, and controllable.
不要用破折号

Recent work has investigated shifting reasoning from discrete token sequences to a continuous latent space, allowing models to *think* with hidden states or learned embeddings rather than emitting lengthy chains of thought. Training LLMs to reason in latent space \citep{hao2024training,shen2025codi,wei2025sim} has been shown to improve both efficiency and flexibility. Extending this idea to multimodal settings, recent methods train MLLMs to align special latent tokens in a continuous semantic space with visual embeddings before generating natural language. These visual embeddings may come from auxiliary helper images \citep{yang2025machine} or annotated bounding boxes \citep{li2025latent}. This alignment transforms “what is seen” into an intermediate, semantically coherent representation that language models can more readily consume, making downstream reasoning more stable, accurate, and controllable.

This alignment transforms \textit{what is seen} into an intermediate, semantically coherent representation that language models can more readily consume, making downstream reasoning more visual grounded.（更遵循视觉信息的，可能coherence？）

可以把重点放在“grounded in visual evidence”上，而不是 coherence。几种更自然的改写如下（无破折号）：

1) **推荐**
This alignment transforms *what is seen* into an intermediate representation that is semantically aligned and easier for language models to consume, thereby grounding downstream reasoning more firmly in visual evidence.

2)
This alignment converts *what is seen* into an intermediate representation that language models can more readily consume, encouraging downstream reasoning to stay more faithful to the visual input.

3)
This alignment turns *what is seen* into a model-consumable intermediate representation, which helps keep subsequent reasoning better anchored to visual information.
These visual embeddings may come from auxiliary helper images \citep{yang2025machine} or annotated bounding boxes \citep{li2025latent}. 经visual encoder投射后的embedding
These visual embeddings are obtained by projecting auxiliary helper images \citep{yang2025machine} or annotated bounding boxes \citep{li2025latent} through a visual encoder.

COVT~\citep{qin2025chain} introduces more fine-grained perception tokens such as depth map and DINO features to enhance visual reasoning.
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.19418 结合文章两句话概括

COVT \citep{qin2025chain} proposes a chain-of-visual-thought framework that performs explicit visual reasoning by operating on structured perception tokens rather than directly generating free-form text. It augments these tokens with fine-grained visual cues, including depth maps and DINO features, which improves grounding and supports more accurate multi-step visual reasoning.
简短

COVT \citep{qin2025chain} performs multi-step visual reasoning by chaining structured perception tokens instead of relying on free-form text. It enriches these tokens with fine-grained cues such as depth maps and DINO features to improve visual grounding and reasoning accuracy.
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.21395 两句话概括
我目前无法直接访问该链接内容。请把论文的标题与摘要（或引言前几段）贴出来，我就能按你的写作风格用两句话精炼概括。

“Thinking with images” has emerged as an effective
paradigm for advancing visual reasoning, extending beyond text-only chains of thought by injecting visual evidence into intermediate reasoning steps. However, existing methods fall short of human-like abstract visual thinking, as their flexibility is fundamentally limited by external tools. In this work, we introduce Monet, a training
framework that enables multimodal large language models
(MLLMs) to reason directly within the latent visual space
by generating continuous embeddings that function as intermediate visual thoughts. We identify two core challenges in
training MLLMs for latent visual reasoning—high computational cost in latent–vision alignment and insufficient supervision over latent embeddings, and address them with a
three-stage distillation-based supervised fine-tuning (SFT)
pipeline. We further reveal a limitation of applying GRPO
to latent reasoning: it primarily enhances text-based reasoning rather than latent reasoning. To overcome this, we
propose VLPO (Visual-latent Policy Optimization), a reinforcement learning method that explicitly incorporates
latent embeddings into policy gradient updates. To support SFT, we construct Monet-SFT-125K, a high-quality
text–image interleaved CoT dataset containing 125K realworld, chart, OCR, and geometry CoTs. Our model,
Monet-7B, shows consistent gains across real-world perception and reasoning benchmarks and exhibits strong outof-distribution generalization on challenging abstract visual reasoning tasks. We also empirically analyze the role
of each training component and discuss our early unsuccessful attempts, providing insights for future developments
in visual latent reasoning. Our model, data, and code are
available at https://github.com/NOVAglow646/
Monet.

Monet is a training framework that enables MLLMs to perform visual reasoning directly in latent visual space by generating continuous embeddings as intermediate “visual thoughts,” rather than relying on external tools. It addresses alignment cost and weak supervision with a three-stage distillation SFT pipeline and further introduces VLPO to explicitly optimize latent embeddings, yielding consistent benchmark gains and strong OOD generalization on abstract visual reasoning.
一句话

Monet trains MLLMs to reason directly in latent visual space by generating continuous “visual thought” embeddings, using a distillation-based SFT pipeline and VLPO to effectively supervise and optimize latent reasoning for stronger grounded and abstract visual generalization.
However, these works only 利用输入LLM前visual encoder编码的视觉特征对latent tokens进行约束，相当于强制让LLM在同一语义空间处理视觉token和语言token , but omit the importance of attention map的引导

However, these works constrain latent tokens only using visual features encoded by a visual encoder before they are fed into the LLM, effectively forcing the LLM to process visual and language tokens within a shared semantic space, while overlooking the importance of guidance from attention maps.
